name: Volo's Guide to the Sword Coast
author: Volothamp Geddarm (annotated by Elminster)
description:
Volothamp Geddarm welcomes the reader to a survey of inns, taverns, shops, and sights along the western Heartlands — from south of Waterdeep to the borders of Amn, and east to Anauroch and Tunland.

The guide divides the Coast into five regions: the Coast proper, the High Moor, the Fields of the Dead, the Backlands, and Sunset Vale. Volo rates establishments with his trademark enthusiasm; marginal notes suggest Elminster has corrected more than a few claims.

name: Volo's Guide to the North
author: Volothamp Geddarm (annotated by Elminster)
description:
The famed travelogue continues north of Waterdeep into the Savage Frontier — a land of long winters, proud city-states, and roads that vanish into monster-haunted wilds.

Volo catalogues settlements from Luskan to Silverymoon, warns of orc holds and Zhentarim ambitions, and repeats traveller's wisdom on when to hire guides and when to simply run. Elminster's footnotes dispute several tavern prices.

name: Volo's Guide to Waterdeep
author: Volothamp Geddarm (annotated by Elminster)
description:
An earlier volume in Volo's celebrated series, devoted entirely to the City of Splendors — its wards, guild politics, famous festhalls, and the unspoken rules of navigating noble society.

Volo praises Waterdeep as the safest great city on the coast while cataloguing a dozen ways an outlander can still lose coin, dignity, or life before breakfast.

name: Volo's Guide to All Things Magical
author: Volothamp Geddarm (annotated by Elminster)
description:
Volo turns from geography to enchantment — a gossip-laden tour of famous magic items, reputed spellbooks, and the eccentrics who collect them across the Heartlands.

The author insists he never steals secrets, only shares what any polite dinner guest might overhear. Several entries end with Elminster's terse corrections and warnings about cursed regalia.

name: Volo's Guide to the Dalelands
author: Volothamp Geddarm (annotated by Elminster)
description:
Volo turns east from the Coast to the Dalelands — feudal quirks, Harper-haunted forests, and the polite fiction that Dalesfolk are simple farmers rather than experienced guerrillas.

Elminster's notes are unusually extensive; several dale-lords reportedly offered bounties for the author's capture.

name: Volo's Guide to Baldur's Gate
author: Volothamp Geddarm
description:
An unpublished manuscript circulated in samizdat — Volo's impressions of the Gate's patriars, Outer City grit, and which Flaming Fist officers accept bribes versus those who shoot first.

Volo insists the work was suppressed by Duke's agents; critics note he never finished the chapter on the Undercellar.

name: Volo's Guide to Cormyr
author: Volothamp Geddarm (annotated by Elminster)
description:
Volo's account of Cormyr's court etiquette, Purple Dragon deployments, and the polite fiction that the Forest Kingdom is less dangerous than the Frontier.

Elminster's footnotes suggest Volo was escorted out of Suzail faster than he entered; the guide's tone is admiring and terrified in equal measure.

name: Volo's Guide to the Moonsea
author: Volothamp Geddarm
description:
Volo's Guide to the Moonsea — A regional survey written for travellers who cannot afford to learn geography from dead companions. Volothamp Geddarm is credited in library catalogues; the work is dated to 1357 DR.

Maps are absent or obsolete in most copies; readers are expected to update the text with their own ink.

name: Volo's Bestiaries: The Malaugrym
author: Volothamp Geddarm
description:
Volo's Bestiaries: The Malaugrym — A treatise on magic as it is actually practiced — wards, reputations, and the price of mispronunciation. Volothamp Geddarm is credited in library catalogues; the work is dated to before 1374 DR.

Complete copies are rare; excerpted versions circulate among mages who insist the missing pages do not matter.
